summary: The agent concludes that phospholipid hydroperoxide glutathone peroxidase (GPX4) is a selenoprotein due to its requirement for selenocysteine at the active site and membership within the selenium-dependent glutathione peroxidase family. This conclusion aligns with established biochemistry showing that all known glutathione peroxidases contain selenocysteine and rely on dietary selenium for function.
